content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a 'skeleton' that can be modularly augmented with multiple inconsistent perspective sampling from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a hybrid self-improvement pipeline that still retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no additional training or supervision. This hybrid approach resolves a key unaddressed limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: its tendency to get stuck in local optimization loops from single-perspective feedback.
